verse_specific_anchors: the four-medium denial structure — each medium named individually (śastra/pāvaka/āpaḥ/māruta) and mapped to a distinct attack-mode (rewrites/fashion-cycles/tech-debt/neglect)

the structural argument that each medium acts through a specific mode of decomposition; the analog preserves this (rewrites cleave, fashion cycles burn, tech debt soaks, neglect dries) rather than collapsing to a single 'durability' claim
the Kubernetes→Nomad scenario tests each medium against named artifacts: CRDs are weapons-cuttable, Helm charts are combustible, ingress controllers drown, cron scripts wither
verdict_reason: I tested against three candidate verses: BG 2.20 (the soul is unborn — overlaps but lacks the four-medium structure), BG 2.24 (the affirmative-property list — names the four denials but adds five more, and the engineering analog there is the nine-property checklist), BG 12.13-19 (the qualities of the devotee — different doctrinal frame entirely). None has the specific four-medium-denial structure. The analog is anchored on 2.23-specific four-element invariance. PASS — not transferable.
